title: Fixtures, Golden Files, Properties, Fuzzing, HTML Validation, and Browser Tests · Language Clinic: Property Tests
summary: Tests snapshot everything, approve changed HTML blindly, omit malformed input, compare only strings, never mutate fixtures, and treat validator success as user-flow proof. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the property tests increment in layered parser-to-browser verification matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-…
objectives: Check consumption, round-trip boundaries, tree validity, escaping, route uniqueness, determinism, clean equivalence, and idempotence across generated cases.
Use small reviewed input, expected tree, diagnostics, route manifest, and output fixtures with explicit update review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Property Tests, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to layered parser-to-browser verification matrix.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-testing-validation-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-testing-validation-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_testing_validation_3(fixture_cases: int = 48, detected_mutants: int = 46, required_mutants: int = 45, validator_errors: int = 0, browser_tasks_failed: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if fixture_cases < 10 or detected_mutants < 0:
        return False, detected_mutants, "test-shape"
    if detected_mutants < required_mutants:
        return False, detected_mutants, "mutation-gap"
    if validator_errors > 0:
        return False, validator_errors, "invalid-output"
    if browser_tasks_failed > 0:
        return False, browser_tasks_failed, "reader-flow-failure"
    assert detected_mutants >= required_mutants
    return True, detected_mutants, "layered-verification"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Property Tests evidence · Theory Property Tests
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-property-tests-1mynqo" that uses case 3164 to combine mutation-sensitive parser, property, validation, and browser evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-property-tests-1mynqo
# Competency: ssg-property-tests.
# Case 3164: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_property_tests_1mynqo(fixture_cases: int = 48, detected_mutants: int = 46, required_mutants: int = 45, validator_errors: int = 0, browser_tasks_failed: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1mynqo = "1mynqo-oqnym1-3164"
    assert evidence_variant_1mynqo
    evidence_axes_1mynqo = (
        "reader1mynqo",
        "source3164",
        "parseoqnym1",
        "route1mynqo3164",
        "artifact31641mynqo",
        "recoveryoqnym13164",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1mynqo)) == 6
    if fixture_cases < 10 or detected_mutants < 0:
        return False, detected_mutants, "test-shape"
    if detected_mutants < required_mutants:
        return False, detected_mutants, "mutation-gap"
    if validator_errors > 0:
        return False, validator_errors, "invalid-output"
    if browser_tasks_failed > 0:
        return False, browser_tasks_failed, "reader-flow-failure"
    assert detected_mutants >= required_mutants
    return True, detected_mutants, "layered-verification"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Property Tests model · Theory Property Tests
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Property Tests: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 7621 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to combine mutation-sensitive parser, property, validation, and browser evidence; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Check consumption, round-trip boundaries, tree validity, escaping, route uniqueness, determinism, clean equivalence, and idempotence across generated cases.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Property Tests model · Theory Property Tests.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Property Tests model · Theory Property Tests, keep Property Tests connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Check consumption, round-trip boundaries, tree validity, escaping, route uniqueness, determinism, clean equivalence, and idempotence across generated cases.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Property Tests model · Theory Property Tests, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Property Tests precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Property Tests model · Theory Property Tests, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 7621 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to combine mutation-sensitive parser, property, validation, and browser evidence; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Check consumption, round-trip boundaries, tree validity, escaping, route uniqueness, determinism, clean equivalence, and idempotence across generated cases.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Property Tests model · Theory Property Tests, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Property testing means generating random Markdown and expecting no exception.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-property-tests-1mynqo.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-property-tests-1mynqo(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_property_tests_1mynqo\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?fixture_cases < 10 or detected_mutants < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?detected_mutants < required_mutants)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?validator_errors > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?browser_tasks_failed > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
